name: Potential Credential Dumping Tool Detected
notes: This rule triggers when a potential credential dumping tool is detected.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log

when an event matches any of the following Suspicious Access to lsass Process, Suspicious Access to lsass Process From Unknown Call Trace, Thread Creation into lsass Process, Mimikatz IMP Hash Observed, Remote Management Service Connected to lsass Pipe, Lsass Process Connected to a Pipe, Unsigned Executable Loaded In lsass
TD_list: APPLY Potential Credential Dumping Tool Detected on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log

AND when an event matches any of the following Suspicious Access to lsass Process, Suspicious Access to lsass Process From Unknown Call Trace, Thread Creation into lsass Process, Mimikatz IMP Hash Observed, Remote Management Service Connected to lsass Pipe, Lsass Process Connected to a Pipe, Unsigned Executable Loaded In lsass
RED_list: Event name: Detected a Possible Credential Dumping Tool
Event description: Detected a Possible Credential Dumping Tool
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
Add the "sourceIP" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "Windows Sensitive Processes"
